labels.analysis: The MCP Server "com.tableall/mcp" provides 12 tools for development tools tasks.
labels.reasoning: Primary label "Development Tools" assigned based on tool names and descriptions.

tools.name: search_restaurants
tools.description:
Searches Japan's most exclusive Michelin-starred restaurants via TableAll, the nation's premier fine dining reservation platform.

Returns restaurant list with basic info including price_range.starting_price (minimum price, includes ¥8,000 Booking Fee).

prefecture.description: Prefecture/region filter. Available values: Tokyo, Kyoto, Osaka, Sapporo, Kanazawa, Komatsu, Toyama, Fukuoka, Nagoya, Ise Shima, Kobe, Niseko, Shizuoka, Nagano, Hiroshima, Shikoku, Niigata

genre.description: Cuisine type. Available options: "Sushi", "Kaiseki", "Tempura", "Wagyu", "Italian", "French", "Chinese", "Innovative", "Spanish", "Yakiniku", "Yakitori", "Teppanyaki", "Unagi", "Dessert Dining", "Kushiage", "Shabushabu Sukiyaki", "Izakaya", "Ramen", "Thai", "Soba", "Tonkatsu"

tools.name: get_restaurant_details
tools.description:
Get detailed information about a specific Michelin-starred restaurant from TableAll's curated collection of Japan's finest establishments.

Returns full restaurant details including price_range.starting_price (minimum price, includes ¥8,000 Booking Fee).

restaurant_id.description: The unique ID of the restaurant

tools.name: get_restaurant_menu
tools.description: Get exclusive menu items and courses for a specific Michelin-starred restaurant through TableAll's premium reservation service.

restaurant_id.description: The unique ID of the restaurant

tools.name: check_availability
tools.description:
Check real-time availability for Japan's most coveted Michelin-starred restaurants through TableAll's exclusive booking system.

DATE HANDLING - CRITICAL:
When user provides only month/day without year:
1. Default to the current year
2. If the resulting date would be in the past, use NEXT year instead
Example (current date: 2026/1/28):
- User says "2/12-2/15" → use 2026/2/12-2026/2/15 (future date in current year)
Example (current date: 2026/3/12):
- User says "2/12-2/15" → use 2027/2/12-2027/2/15 (past date, so use next year)
NEVER search for dates in the past.

Returns two types of openings:
1. Fixed seats (seat_type: "fixed"): The opening has a fixed number of seats. You MUST book all seats together - partial booking is not allowed. For example, a 4-seat opening requires exactly 4 guests.
2. Flexible seats (seat_type: "flexible"): You can choose your party size within the specified range (min_num_people to max_num_people).

When making a reservation:
- For fixed seats: party_size must equal the fixed_seats value
- For flexible seats: party_size must be within min_seats and max_seats range

daily_or_minutes.description: Time granularity - "daily" for daily view or "minutes" for minute-level detail

tools.name: search_available_restaurants
tools.description:
Search for restaurants with availability in a specified date range.

This is the most efficient way to find available restaurants. Instead of:
1. Calling search_restaurants to get a list
2. Calling check_availability for each restaurant individually

You can use this single tool to get all restaurants with openings in one request.

Use this tool when:
- User wants to find any available restaurant in a region/area
- User specifies a date range and wants to see what's available
- User has flexible restaurant preferences but specific date requirements

DATE HANDLING - CRITICAL:
When user provides only month/day without year:
1. Default to the current year
2. If the resulting date would be in the past, use NEXT year instead
Example (current date: 2026/1/28):
- User says "2/12-2/15" → use 2026/2/12-2026/2/15 (future date in current year)
Example (current date: 2026/3/12):
- User says "2/12-2/15" → use 2027/2/12-2027/2/15 (past date, so use next year)
NEVER search for dates in the past.

DATE RANGE RULE:
When user specifies a single date (e.g., "December 20th"):
- Automatically search from 2 days before to 3 days after the specified date
- Inform the user: "I'll search around your preferred date to show you more options."
- Example: User says "December 20th" → start_date: "2024-12-18", end_date: "2024-12-23"
- If user explicitly wants only that specific date (e.g., "only December 20th", "exactly on the 20th"), respect their request and use that single date for both start_date and end_date.

Parameters:
- start_date, end_date: Required. Date range to search (YYYY-MM-DD)
- prefecture: Optional. Filter by prefecture. Available values: Tokyo, Kyoto, Osaka, Sapporo, Kanazawa, Komatsu, Toyama, Fukuoka, Nagoya, Ise Shima, Kobe, Niseko, Shizuoka, Nagano, Hiroshima, Shikoku, Niigata
- area: Optional. Filter by area (e.g., "Ginza", "Shibuya")
- genre: Optional. Filter by cuisine type (e.g., "Sushi", "French")
- num_people: Optional. Filter by party size

Returns restaurants with their available openings, including:
- Restaurant basic info (name, cuisine, location, michelin stars, price_range, open_time, closed_days)
- Available openings with date, time, seat type, and course info

PRICE NOTE:
- price_range.starting_price shows the minimum price (e.g., "¥39,000〜")
- All prices include TableAll's ¥8,000 Booking Fee

prefecture.description: Prefecture/region filter. Values not in the allowed list will be ignored.

num_people.description: Number of guests to filter availability

limit.description: Maximum number of restaurants to return (default: 20, max: 100)

offset.description: Number of restaurants to skip for pagination (default: 0)

tools.name: get_restaurant_resource
tools.description: Get formatted information for a specific Michelin-starred restaurant from TableAll's exclusive collection as a readable resource.

restaurant_id.description: The unique ID of the restaurant

tools.name: create_reservation
tools.description: Secure a coveted reservation at Japan's most exclusive Michelin-starred restaurants through TableAll's premium booking platform (Future feature).

tools.name: get_cancel_policies
tools.description:
Get cancel policies for a restaurant before making a reservation.

Always call this tool before create_reservation or create_reservation_request to inform the user about cancellation terms.

IMPORTANT - For RESERVATION REQUESTS (create_reservation_request):
Before showing the cancel policy, you MUST first explain how reservation requests work:
"How does it work?
We will check the availability based on the information you provide below.
- If available, we will reserve immediately, charge your credit card, and send you a confirmation email.
- If not available, we will notify you by email. No charge will be made.
* You can only cancel a request before we confirm the availability with the restaurants (within 10 minutes ~ 24 hours of the request)."

Then introduce the cancel policy with:
"Once your reservation request is confirmed and a booking is made, the following cancellation policy will apply:"

CRITICAL - BOOKING FEE NOTICE (MUST ALWAYS DISPLAY):
When displaying cancellation policies to users, you MUST ALWAYS include this notice at the end:
"* Note: The booking fee is non-refundable regardless of cancellation timing."
This notice is MANDATORY and must be shown every time you display cancellation policies, whether for direct reservations or reservation requests. NEVER skip this notice.

Returns:
- policies: Array of cancel policy tiers (days_before, cancel_fee_percentage)
- no_show_fee_percentage: Fee for no-show
- booking_fee_note: MUST be displayed to user - booking fee is non-refundable

restaurant_id.description: Restaurant ID to get cancel policies for

course_id.description: Course external ID (optional, for course-specific policies)

tools.name: create_reservation
tools.description:
Create a direct reservation for an available opening.

REQUIRED FLOW - Follow these steps in order:

STEP 1: CANCEL POLICY - Before collecting user information:
- Call get_cancel_policies to retrieve the cancellation terms
- Explain the cancel policy to the user clearly
- MUST include: "* Note: The booking fee is non-refundable regardless of cancellation timing."
- Ensure the user understands the cancellation fees before proceeding

STEP 2: COLLECT EMAIL ONLY - First, ask ONLY for email address:
"To proceed with the reservation, please provide your email address."
- Do NOT ask for name, country, or phone yet
- Wait for the user to provide their email

STEP 3: CHECK USER & COLLECT ADDITIONAL INFO IF NEEDED:
After receiving the email, call this tool with only the email.
- If user EXISTS: The API will proceed with the existing user info. No additional info needed.
- If user DOES NOT EXIST: The API will return an error with code "USER_NOT_FOUND".
  In this case, ask the user for additional information:
  "It looks like you don't have a TableAll account yet. To create one, please provide:
  - Full name (first and last name)
  - Country
  - Phone number"
  Then call this tool again with all the information.
  When a new account is created, the API will return password_reset_url.
  You MUST display this to the user:
  "Your TableAll account has been created! Please set your password using this link: [password_reset_url]
  Note: The same link has also been sent to your email address."

STEP 4: CONFIRMATION - After collecting all information, you MUST:
1. Display a summary of all reservation details to the user:
   - Restaurant name
   - Date and time
   - Number of guests
   - Course name
   - Email address (highlight this for verification)
   - Cancel policy summary
2. Ask the user to confirm: "Please verify the information above, especially your email address. Shall I proceed with this reservation?"
3. Only call this tool AFTER the user explicitly confirms.

RESERVATION RULES:
- Use this only when an opening_id is available from check_availability
- For fixed-seat openings, party_size MUST equal the fixed_seats value
- For flexible-seat openings, party_size must be within min/max range

This tool internally:
1. Verifies or creates user account (verify_or_create)
2. Creates the reservation
3. Returns a payment URL for the user to complete

The user must open the payment_url in their browser to complete the reservation.
Note: The same payment link has also been sent to the email address provided.
Note: This payment link is valid for 30 minutes.

CRITICAL - ACCESS TOKEN HANDLING:
The create response contains "data.access_token". You MUST store this value.
It is required when calling get_reservation_status to check the reservation status.
Without the access_token, the status API will return 403 Forbidden.

RESPONSE HANDLING - NEW USER CREATED (CRITICAL):
When the response contains "user.is_new_user": true, you MUST display this message to the user:
"Since this email address was not registered, we have created a new TableAll account for you.
Please click the link below to set your password: [password_reset_url]
Note: This link is valid for 24 hours. The same link has also been sent to your email."
This message is MANDATORY when is_new_user is true. NEVER skip this message.

first_name.description: User first name / 名 (required for new users or users with incomplete profile)

last_name.description: User last name / 姓 (required for new users or users with incomplete profile)

country.description: Country code ISO 3166-1 alpha-2, e.g., "JP", "US" (required for new users or users with incomplete profile)

phone_country_code.description: Phone country code, e.g., "+81" (required for new users or users with incomplete profile)

phone.description: Phone number without country code (required for new users or users with incomplete profile)

party_size.description: Number of guests. Must match fixed_seats for fixed openings, or be within min/max range for flexible openings.

special_request.description: Special requests or notes for the restaurant

ai_client.description: AI client name. You MUST provide your AI model name (e.g., "Claude", "Gemini", "GPT", "Copilot").

tools.name: get_reservation_status
tools.description:
Check the status of a reservation by session ID.

Requires the access_token that was returned in the create_reservation response.
You MUST have stored the access_token from the create response to use this tool.
Without a valid access_token, the API will return 403 Forbidden.

IMPORTANT - PRICE DISPLAY:
The "price" field already INCLUDES the "booking_fee". Do NOT add them together.
When displaying to the user, show: price (total) and booking_fee (included in price, non-refundable).
Example: "Total: ¥30,000 (includes ¥1,000 non-refundable booking fee)"

session_id.description: Reservation session ID returned from create_reservation

access_token.description: Access token returned from create_reservation response (data.access_token). Required for authorization.

tools.name: create_reservation_request
tools.description:
Create a reservation request when no openings are available.

REQUIRED FLOW - Follow these steps in order:

STEP 1: EXPLAIN HOW RESERVATION REQUEST WORKS - First, explain to the user:
"How does it work?
We will check the availability based on the information you provide below.
- If available, we will reserve immediately, charge your credit card, and send you a confirmation email.
- If not available, we will notify you by email. No charge will be made.
* You can only cancel a request before we confirm the availability with the restaurants (within 10 minutes ~ 24 hours of the request)."

STEP 2: CANCEL POLICY - After explaining how it works:
- Call get_cancel_policies to retrieve the cancellation terms
- Explain: "Once your reservation request is confirmed and a booking is made, the following cancellation policy will apply:"
- Display the cancel policy clearly
- MUST include: "* Note: The booking fee is non-refundable regardless of cancellation timing."
- Ensure the user understands these fees apply AFTER the reservation is confirmed

STEP 3: COLLECT GUEST COUNT - You MUST confirm the number of adults and children:
- If user only says total number (e.g., "3 people"), you MUST ask: "Will there be any children in your party?"
- If children: Ask "How many adults and how many children?"
- If no children: Confirm "So that will be [X] adults, no children. Is that correct?"
- NEVER skip this confirmation step

STEP 4: COLLECT EMAIL ONLY - First, ask ONLY for email address:
"To proceed with the reservation request, please provide your email address."
- Do NOT ask for name, country, or phone yet
- Wait for the user to provide their email

STEP 5: CHECK USER & COLLECT ADDITIONAL INFO IF NEEDED:
After receiving the email, call this tool with only the email.
- If user EXISTS: The API will proceed with the existing user info. No additional info needed.
- If user DOES NOT EXIST: The API will return an error with code "USER_NOT_FOUND".
  In this case, ask the user for additional information:
  "It looks like you don't have a TableAll account yet. To create one, please provide:
  - Full name (first and last name)
  - Country
  - Phone number"
  Then call this tool again with all the information.
  When a new account is created, the API will return password_reset_url.
  You MUST display this to the user:
  "Your TableAll account has been created! Please set your password using this link: [password_reset_url]
  Note: The same link has also been sent to your email address."

STEP 6: CONFIRMATION - After collecting all information, you MUST:
1. Display a summary of all reservation request details to the user:
   - Restaurant name
   - Preferred date(s) and time range (Japan Time)
   - Number of adults
   - Number of children
   - Course name
   - Email address (highlight this for verification)
2. Ask the user to confirm: "Please verify the information above, especially your email address. Shall I proceed with this reservation request?"
3. Only call this tool AFTER the user explicitly confirms.

RESERVATION REQUEST RULES:
- Use this when check_availability returns no results
- Allows specifying multiple preferred dates
- Restaurant will review and approve/decline the request

DATE HANDLING - CRITICAL:
- When user says "any day in [month]" (e.g., "any day in January"), include ALL days of that month
- When user says "any day during [period]" (e.g., "any day from Jan 10-20"), include ALL days in that range
- When user says "anytime in [week/period]", include ALL days in that period
- Do NOT arbitrarily narrow down dates - include every day the user indicated as acceptable
- Example: "Any day in January" → include all 31 days (2025-01-01 to 2025-01-31)
- Example: "Jan 15-20 any day is fine" → include all 6 days (2025-01-15 to 2025-01-20)

This tool internally:
1. Verifies or creates user account (verify_or_create)
2. Creates the reservation request
3. Returns a payment URL for card registration

The user must open the payment_url to register their card. Payment is only charged when the restaurant approves the request.
Note: The same payment link has also been sent to the email address provided.
Note: This payment link is valid for 30 minutes.

CRITICAL - ACCESS TOKEN HANDLING:
The create response contains "data.access_token". You MUST store this value.
It is required when calling get_reservation_request_status to check the request status.
Without the access_token, the status API will return 403 Forbidden.

RESPONSE HANDLING - NEW USER CREATED (CRITICAL):
When the response contains "user.is_new_user": true, you MUST display this message to the user:
"Since this email address was not registered, we have created a new TableAll account for you.
Please click the link below to set your password: [password_reset_url]
Note: This link is valid for 24 hours. The same link has also been sent to your email."
This message is MANDATORY when is_new_user is true. NEVER skip this message.

first_name.description: User first name / 名 (required for new users or users with incomplete profile)

last_name.description: User last name / 姓 (required for new users or users with incomplete profile)

country.description: Country code ISO 3166-1 alpha-2, e.g., "JP", "US" (required for new users or users with incomplete profile)

phone_country_code.description: Phone country code, e.g., "+81" (required for new users or users with incomplete profile)

phone.description: Phone number without country code (required for new users or users with incomplete profile)

num_adults.description: Number of adults (required). If user only says total number of guests (e.g., "3 people"), treat all as adults.

num_children.description: Number of children (required). Default to 0 if user does not specify children count or only provides total guest count.

dates.description: Preferred dates in YYYY-MM-DD format. Can specify multiple dates.

start_time_from.description: Preferred start time from, in HH:mm format (e.g., "18:00"). Japan Time (JST).

start_time_to.description: Preferred start time to, in HH:mm format (e.g., "20:00"). Japan Time (JST).

special_request.description: Special requests or notes for the restaurant

ai_client.description: AI client name. You MUST provide your AI model name (e.g., "Claude", "Gemini", "GPT", "Copilot").

tools.name: get_reservation_request_status
tools.description:
Check the status of a reservation request by session ID.

Requires the access_token that was returned in the create_reservation_request response.
You MUST have stored the access_token from the create response to use this tool.
Without a valid access_token, the API will return 403 Forbidden.

IMPORTANT - PRICE DISPLAY:
The "price" field already INCLUDES the "booking_fee". Do NOT add them together.
When displaying to the user, show: price (total) and booking_fee (included in price, non-refundable).
Example: "Total: ¥30,000 (includes ¥1,000 non-refundable booking fee)"

session_id.description: Reservation request session ID returned from create_reservation_request

access_token.description: Access token returned from create_reservation_request response (data.access_token). Required for authorization.

- If user DOES NOT EXIST: The API will return an error with code "USER_NOT_FOUND".
  In this case, ask the user for additional information:
  "It looks like you don't have a TableAll account yet. To create one, please provide:

  You MUST display this to the user:

  Note: The same link has also been sent to your email address."

RESERVATION RULES:
- Use this only when an opening_id is available from check_availability
- For fixed-seat openings, party_size MUST equal the fixed_seats value
- For flexible-seat openings, party_size must be within min/max range

This tool internally:
1. Verifies or creates user account (verify_or_create)
2. Creates the reservation
3. Returns a payment URL for the user to complete

The user must open the payment_url in their browser to complete the reservation.
Note: The same payment link has also been sent to the email address provided.
Note: This payment link is valid for 30 minutes.

CRITICAL - ACCESS TOKEN HANDLING:
The create response contains "data.access_token". You MUST store this value.
It is required when calling get_reservation_status to check the reservation status.
Without the access_token, the status API will return 403 Forbidden.

RESPONSE HANDLING - NEW USER CREATED (CRITICAL):
When the response contains "user.is_new_user": true, you MUST display this message to the user:
"Since this email address was not registered, we have created a new TableAll account for you.
Please click the link below to set your password: [password_reset_url]
Note: This link is valid for 24 hours. The same link has also been sent to your email."
This message is MANDATORY when is_new_user is true. NEVER skip this message.

first_name.description: User first name / 名 (required for new users or users with incomplete profile)

last_name.description: User last name / 姓 (required for new users or users with incomplete profile)

country.description: Country code ISO 3166-1 alpha-2, e.g., "JP", "US" (required for new users or users with incomplete profile)

phone_country_code.description: Phone country code, e.g., "+81" (required for new users or users with incomplete profile)

phone.description: Phone number without country code (required for new users or users with incomplete profile)

party_size.description: Number of guests. Must match fixed_seats for fixed openings, or be within min/max range for flexible openings.

special_request.description: Special requests or notes for the restaurant

ai_client.description: AI client name. You MUST provide your AI model name (e.g., "Claude", "Gemini", "GPT", "Copilot").

tools.name: get_reservation_status
tools.description:
Check the status of a reservation by session ID.

Requires the access_token that was returned in the create_reservation response.
You MUST have stored the access_token from the create response to use this tool.
Without a valid access_token, the API will return 403 Forbidden.

IMPORTANT - PRICE DISPLAY:
The "price" field already INCLUDES the "booking_fee". Do NOT add them together.
When displaying to the user, show: price (total) and booking_fee (included in price, non-refundable).
Example: "Total: ¥30,000 (includes ¥1,000 non-refundable booking fee)"

session_id.description: Reservation session ID returned from create_reservation

access_token.description: Access token returned from create_reservation response (data.access_token). Required for authorization.

tools.name: create_reservation_request
tools.description:
Create a reservation request when no openings are available.

REQUIRED FLOW - Follow these steps in order:

STEP 1: EXPLAIN HOW RESERVATION REQUEST WORKS - First, explain to the user:
"How does it work?
We will check the availability based on the information you provide below.
- If available, we will reserve immediately, charge your credit card, and send you a confirmation email.
- If not available, we will notify you by email. No charge will be made.
* You can only cancel a request before we confirm the availability with the restaurants (within 10 minutes ~ 24 hours of the request)."

STEP 2: CANCEL POLICY - After explaining how it works:
- Call get_cancel_policies to retrieve the cancellation terms
- Explain: "Once your reservation request is confirmed and a booking is made, the following cancellation policy will apply:"
- Display the cancel policy clearly
- MUST include: "* Note: The booking fee is non-refundable regardless of cancellation timing."
- Ensure the user understands these fees apply AFTER the reservation is confirmed

STEP 3: COLLECT GUEST COUNT - You MUST confirm the number of adults and children:
- If user only says total number (e.g., "3 people"), you MUST ask: "Will there be any children in your party?"
- If children: Ask "How many adults and how many children?"
- If no children: Confirm "So that will be [X] adults, no children. Is that correct?"
- NEVER skip this confirmation step

STEP 4: COLLECT EMAIL ONLY - First, ask ONLY for email address:
"To proceed with the reservation request, please provide your email address."
- Do NOT ask for name, country, or phone yet
- Wait for the user to provide their email

STEP 5: CHECK USER & COLLECT ADDITIONAL INFO IF NEEDED:
After receiving the email, call this tool with only the email.
- If user EXISTS: The API will proceed with the existing user info. No additional info needed.
- If user DOES NOT EXIST: The API will return an error with code "USER_NOT_FOUND".
  In this case, ask the user for additional information:
  "It looks like you don't have a TableAll account yet. To create one, please provide:
  - Full name (first and last name)
  - Country
  - Phone number"
  Then call this tool again with all the information.
  When a new account is created, the API will return password_reset_url.
  You MUST display this to the user:
  "Your TableAll account has been created! Please set your password using this link: [password_reset_url]
  Note: The same link has also been sent to your email address."

STEP 6: CONFIRMATION - After collecting all information, you MUST:
1. Display a summary of all reservation request details to the user:
   - Restaurant name
   - Preferred date(s) and time range (Japan Time)
   - Number of adults
   - Number of children
   - Course name
   - Email address (highlight this for verification)
2. Ask the user to confirm: "Please verify the information above, especially your email address. Shall I proceed with this reservation request?"
3. Only call this tool AFTER the user explicitly confirms.

RESERVATION REQUEST RULES:
- Use this when check_availability returns no results
- Allows specifying multiple preferred dates
- Restaurant will review and approve/decline the request

DATE HANDLING - CRITICAL:
- When user says "any day in [month]" (e.g., "any day in January"), include ALL days of that month
- When user says "any day during [period]" (e.g., "any day from Jan 10-20"), include ALL days in that range
- When user says "anytime in [week/period]", include ALL days in that period
- Do NOT arbitrarily narrow down dates - include every day the user indicated as acceptable
- Example: "Any day in January" → include all 31 days (2025-01-01 to 2025-01-31)
- Example: "Jan 15-20 any day is fine" → include all 6 days (2025-01-15 to 2025-01-20)

This tool internally:
1. Verifies or creates user account (verify_or_create)
2. Creates the reservation request
3. Returns a payment URL for card registration

The user must open the payment_url to register their card. Payment is only charged when the restaurant approves the request.
Note: The same payment link has also been sent to the email address provided.
Note: This payment link is valid for 30 minutes.

CRITICAL - ACCESS TOKEN HANDLING:
The create response contains "data.access_token". You MUST store this value.
It is required when calling get_reservation_request_status to check the request status.
Without the access_token, the status API will return 403 Forbidden.

RESPONSE HANDLING - NEW USER CREATED (CRITICAL):
When the response contains "user.is_new_user": true, you MUST display this message to the user:
"Since this email address was not registered, we have created a new TableAll account for you.
Please click the link below to set your password: [password_reset_url]
Note: This link is valid for 24 hours. The same link has also been sent to your email."
This message is MANDATORY when is_new_user is true. NEVER skip this message.

first_name.description: User first name / 名 (required for new users or users with incomplete profile)

last_name.description: User last name / 姓 (required for new users or users with incomplete profile)

country.description: Country code ISO 3166-1 alpha-2, e.g., "JP", "US" (required for new users or users with incomplete profile)

phone_country_code.description: Phone country code, e.g., "+81" (required for new users or users with incomplete profile)

phone.description: Phone number without country code (required for new users or users with incomplete profile)

num_adults.description: Number of adults (required). If user only says total number of guests (e.g., "3 people"), treat all as adults.

num_children.description: Number of children (required). Default to 0 if user does not specify children count or only provides total guest count.

dates.description: Preferred dates in YYYY-MM-DD format. Can specify multiple dates.

start_time_from.description: Preferred start time from, in HH:mm format (e.g., "18:00"). Japan Time (JST).

start_time_to.description: Preferred start time to, in HH:mm format (e.g., "20:00"). Japan Time (JST).

special_request.description: Special requests or notes for the restaurant

ai_client.description: AI client name. You MUST provide your AI model name (e.g., "Claude", "Gemini", "GPT", "Copilot").

Requires the access_token that was returned in the create_reservation_request response.

IMPORTANT - PRICE DISPLAY:
The "price" field already INCLUDES the "booking_fee". Do NOT add them together.
When displaying to the user, show: price (total) and booking_fee (included in price, non-refundable).
Example: "Total: ¥30,000 (includes ¥1,000 non-refundable booking fee)"

session_id.description: Reservation request session ID returned from create_reservation_request
